title: XINYU ZHANG
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: XINYU ZHANG
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)
 





🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)










💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
Visiting NARIT was a really unforgettable experience for me. The planetarium is a three-floor building, and the first floor has a huge ball-shaped theater. When I watched the show inside, I felt like I was floating in real space, surrounded by stars and planets. I also saw a very big telescope in the building, which was really eye-catching. This trip made me feel that the universe is so wonderful and mysterious, and it made me more interested in science and technology.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
During the visit, I learned many basic ideas about light and heat. For example, I learned how planets reflect light from the sun, and how heat is transferred from stars to other objects in space. These scientific principles were not just boring words in textbooks— they became real and easy to understand when I saw the models and videos at NARIT. I also learned about how telescopes work to help us see faraway things in the universe.


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
As a student studying computer science, this trip made me think about how I can use my knowledge in the future. I realized that computer programs can be used to simulate the movement of stars and planets, just like the models I saw at NARIT. In the future, I hope to use coding skills to help with space research or create educational apps that make science more fun for other people. This visit encouraged me to study harder and explore more about the world and the universe.

title: YUEYU TIAN
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: YUEYU TIAN
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)






🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)










💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
Visiting NARIT felt like stepping into a space where science becomes alive and immersive. The planetarium experience, interactive exhibits, and astronomical displays made complex concepts feel accessible and exciting. Instead of just reading about space, I was able to experience it visually and emotionally, which made the visit both inspiring and memorable.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
Through this visit, I learned how planetarium projection systems simulate celestial movements and recreate realistic night skies. I also gained a deeper understanding of black holes, light spectra, and how telescopes collect and analyze data from distant galaxies. The integration of technology and science communication was particularly impressive.


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
This experience showed me how scientific knowledge can be transformed into engaging educational content. In the future, I can apply these insights to improve presentation skills, integrate interactive elements into projects, and develop more experience-based learning approaches within my field.

title: KITKWIN PHIANCHOB
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: KITKWIN PHIANCHOB
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)






🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)










💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
The visit transformed astronomy from just a theory-based subject into something tangible and meaningful. Seeing how scientists observe and interpret light helped me understand how space knowledge is actually gathered. I appreciated how the exhibits combined curiosity with scientific discipline through real tools, step-by-step analysis, and logical explanations. It made astronomy feel achievable rather than distant, and it inspired me to explore more.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
I learned how light can be used as evidence to understand space objects, including their position, movement, and characteristics. I also discovered how observation depends on proper instruments, settings, and environmental conditions. Most importantly, I realized that astronomy is built on data collection and interpretation rather than simple guessing.


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
In the future, I can apply this knowledge by observing the night sky more systematically using sky-mapping apps and recording details such as time, brightness, and location. I could also create a small digital project explaining how observations turn into scientific conclusions. This experience encouraged me to think critically by always asking, “How do we know?” when studying scientific information.

title: NARIT Expedition Report
text: Welcome to our group’s trip report. Here, you will find our NARIT documentary and a summary of our activities from an astro-tourism perspective.

title: NARIT Expedition Report
text:
🎥 NARIT Documentary


“Why Teenagers Should Visit NARIT”
According to the assignment, our vlog focuses on highlighting favorite zones and recommended photo spots for students.







📝 Activity Report

1. Observation Deck Exploration
We examined the observation platform, focusing on its educational function for ordinary tourists.


2. Interactive Astronomy Exhibition
We conducted a simulated experience in the interactive exhibition area and assessed its tourism experience value.


3. AstroPark Facilities
As part of “astronomical tourism,” we evaluated its supporting facilities。

title: RONG WEN
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: RONG WEN
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)






🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)

According to the assignment, our vlog focuses on highlighting favorite zones and recommended photo spots for students.











💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
The visit made astronomy feel real and practical. The exhibits helped me connect ideas like light and observation to what scientists actually measure. I liked the balance between excitement and scientific method: tools, careful checks, and clear explanations. Overall, it motivated me to learn the sky with a more data-driven mindset.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
Observation means recording measurable data (light, time, position). Instruments and settings change what details we can detect. Asking ‘how do we know?’ improves scientific thinking


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
I will use a sky map app to identify constellations and planets and keep a simple weekly “sky log” (date, location, what I saw)

title: RUITONG LI
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: RUITONG LI
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)






🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)










💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
Visiting NARIT left a deep impression on me. Its spacious area and modern architecture made the entire visit both relaxing and enjoyable, allowing me to learn and experience things at a leisurely pace. The planetarium also showed me how science and technology help humanity understand the universe.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
From this visit, I learned some basic knowledge about astronomy. I also learned that science centers can use space design and simple technology to help people understand science more easily.


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
This area is suitable for different groups of people. It has elevators and wheelchair friendly paths for elderly visitors and people with disabilities, many seats for resting, and slides for children to play. In the future, this experience can help me think about how public spaces should be designed for everyone.

title: SIYU WANG
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: SIYU WANG
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)






🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)
Here’s another highlight from our NARIT experience.










💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
The planetarium has a beautiful environment and features a wide range of scientific models inside. Visitors can observe many fascinating physical phenomena up close, making it a truly novel and memorable experience.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
By visiting the planetarium, we learned a wide range of knowledge, including but not limited to how celestial bodies move, how light behaves in the universe, what black holes look like, and how humans have understood and studied the stars throughout history. There were also some fascinating elements, and my personal favorite was learning what the universe might sound like.


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
Astronomical knowledge does not remain only at a theoretical level; it is practically applied in satellite navigation, space technology, and the observation of Earth’s environment, and it holds real significance for the future development of humanity.

title: CHENFEI LIU
text:
🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: CHENFEI LIU
Faculty: [ICDI] (Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)





📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)






🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)










💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
Visiting NARIT felt like stepping into a different universe. As a “travel blogger” for the day, I was amazed by how the space exhibitions and planetarium combined science with immersive storytelling. The atmosphere was both educational and inspiring, making astronomy feel exciting rather than distant or complicated.


💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
During the visit, I learned how the planetarium projection system simulates celestial movements and recreates realistic night skies. I also gained a better understanding of black holes, light spectra, and how telescopes collect and process astronomical data to study distant galaxies.


🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
This experience broadened my perspective on how science can be communicated in creative and engaging ways. In the future, I can apply these insights to improve presentation skills, integrate technology into learning, and develop more interactive and experience-based projects within my field.
